danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile organisms with distinct silhouettes; appear as obstacles in side-scrolling levels
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat requiring combat or avoidance; defeating them grants evolution resources

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, more detailed creature sprites at level ends; visually distinct from standard enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Major progression gates; significantly more dangerous than standard foes; require sustained effort to overcome

danger_signals.visual_form: Organisms visually corrupted/monstrously deformed by mysterious crystals; unnatural appearance
danger_signals.meaning: Destructive forces disrupting natural evolution; represent external malevolent interference with world order

danger_signals.visual_form: Period-specific obstacles varying by geological era (aquatic, terrestrial, climate-specific)
danger_signals.meaning: Era-appropriate dangers; necessitate adaptive evolution and survival strategies

danger_signals.visual_form: Upper status bar displaying health points; visual indicator during damage sequences
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to character survival; triggers revival mechanic with evolution point penalty

safety_signals.visual_form: Meat from defeated enemies; nearby plant life; visually distinct collectibles
safety_signals.meaning: Recovery mechanics; restores health; enables survival continuation

safety_signals.visual_form: Special crystals with distinct visual presentation; appear throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Guidance and assistance; safe interaction points providing strategic information

safety_signals.visual_form: Special crystals granting unique body forms; temporary power-up visual effect
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability or enhanced capability; safe power boost during dangerous sections

safety_signals.visual_form: Narrative element; character revival mechanic visualization
safety_signals.meaning: Benevolent protection; no permanent death; compassionate guiding force ensuring player survival

safety_signals.visual_form: Overworld/era-specific map layout; structured level navigation interface
safety_signals.meaning: Safe respite point; allows progression planning and save functionality

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Garden of Eden; mystical light/ethereal visual qualities
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Each geological period represents Gaia's domain; natural creatures inhabit era-appropriate environments

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mysterious crystals; unnatural visual distortion of creatures
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Creatures transformed by crystals appear monstrously deformed and visually corrupted; freakish appearance distinct from natural organisms
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Strange non-native crystals appearing across planet; environmental corruption signaling external invasion

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crystal technology; advanced artificial construct design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Crystal-transformed creatures serve as proxies; alien technology manifests through biological corruption
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Crystals represent extraterrestrial intrusion; non-native to Earth's natural systems

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Aquatic environment; water-based level design; fish and aquatic organism sprites; oceanic color palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player character limited to aquatic body forms; movement and evolution options tied to aquatic physiology; enemies are water-dwelling creatures

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Carboniferous Period - Early Creatures of Land
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Terrestrial environment transitioning from water; primitive plant life; early amphibian/reptile creatures; swamp/forest aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player upgrades to land-capable forms; encounters early land animals; evolution options reflect terrestrial adaptation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Prehistoric landscape; large reptilian creature sprites; dense vegetation; era-specific color grading
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Encounters with dinosaur-class enemies; evolution toward larger, more powerful forms; boss encounters scaled to era's megafauna

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Frozen/glacial environment; snow/ice visual effects; mammalian creature designs; cold color palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player character shifts to mammalian evolution tree; encounters ice-adapted predators; remaining dinosaurs visually appear as relics

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Early Quaternary Period - Age of Man/Pre-Eden
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Early humanoid forms; simpler environments; proto-human creature design; visual progression toward human intelligence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player evolution culminates in near-human forms; encounters final bosses including Bolbox; environment reflects emergence of intelligence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mysterious crystals scattered throughout environments; visually corrupted/deformed creatures; unnatural color shifts in affected areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Crystal-fed creatures become significantly more dangerous; disrupted natural enemy behavior; acceleration toward catastrophic extinction patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Paradise realm visualization; transcendent environment; Gaia's presence made manifest in visual form
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player granted intelligence and partnership with Gaia; peaceful state; culmination of evolutionary narrative

environmental_storytelling.example: Each level visually represents actual historical epochs (Cambrian, Ordovician, Carboniferous, Mesozoic, Neogene, Quaternary); environment design teaches Earth history through visual immersion
Boss creatures and standard enemies reflect each era's dominant lifeforms; visual progression from simple aquatic forms to complex terrestrial organisms tells evolution story without dialogue
Mysterious crystals' visual presence and effect on creatures communicates external threat; unnatural deformation signals non-native destructive force
Level design and creature placement reflect era-specific survival challenges; visual hazards (water, terrain, climate) teach why certain evolutions become necessary
Player character's visual transformation from simple fish to complex mammal to proto-human reflects learning curve and evolutionary advancement through design feedback
Gaia's narrative framing and revival mechanic communicate divine oversight; no game-over state creates visual/mechanical sense of maternal protection throughout adventure

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Engage player character with varied attack patterns (biting, ramming, jumping); attempt to deal damage; serve as resource sources when defeated
Remain at level endpoints; require significantly more effort to defeat; display enhanced combat complexity; serve as progression gates
Visually deformed behavior; monstrously powerful; dominate other lifeforms; overconsume resources; actively disrupt natural evolutionary patterns
Manipulates world's other lifeforms; sends creatures against player; controls environment as ancient single-celled organism in advanced form; believes itself first human
Revived by Gaia; sent back to map screen; loses approximately half accumulated evolution points; no permanent death state
Guides player through geological epochs; resurrects defeated player; selects player for Eden partnership competition; communicates through narrative/mechanical design

notes: E.V.O. communicates world-state primarily through environmental design and creature visual language rather than explicit UI systems. The core manifestation pattern is evolution-as-communication: creature appearance, ability changes, and environmental adaptation requirements all communicate what era the player occupies and what threats/opportunities exist. Danger is signaled through creature deformation (crystals) and scale/visual complexity (bosses). Safety is implicit in resource availability and Gaia's protective mechanical framework. Political situation (Gaia vs. Bolbox vs. Martian interference) is conveyed entirely through narrative context and creature behavior rather than factional visual markers like armor/colors/insignia typical of traditional RPGs. The game uses a 'world teaches through environment' design philosophy.
